title: Democrats Flip Trump's Own Backyard in Stunning Florida Upset
description: Democrat Emily Gregory defeats Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples in Florida's District 87 special election, flipping a seat the GOP won by 19 points in 2024.
lede: A first-time Democratic candidate has won a special election in the Florida state house district that contains Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate, flipping a seat that Republicans won by 19 percentage points just two years ago. The result, in Trump's literal backyard, is being read as a warning shot ahead of November's midterms.

sections.heading: The Numbers Don't Lie
paragraphs: Emily Gregory, a public health expert and small business owner, defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples by more than two percentage points in Florida's 87th state house district. The Associated Press called the race on Tuesday evening. In 2024, a Republican won this same seat by a 19-point margin — making the swing one of the largest special-election reversals in recent Florida history.

Gregory, who launched her campaign nine months ago, told MSNBC she had done 'some crazy calculus' to identify the district as a flip opportunity. 'My math worked,' she said. The result makes her one of a growing number of Democrats who have won upset victories in special elections since Trump's second term began.

sections.heading: Trump Backed His Neighbor — By Mail
paragraphs: Trump personally endorsed Maples on Truth Social, urging 'great patriots' to vote. Republican Congressman Byron Donalds, now running for governor, filmed a campaign endorsement alongside the candidate. The full weight of the GOP establishment was behind this race.

In a detail first reported by The Guardian, Trump voted in the special election via mail-in ballot — despite spending the same week attacking mail-in voting as 'mail-in cheating' during an event in Tennessee. The president has spent years calling the practice a scam, yet used it himself even though early in-person voting was available in Palm Beach until the Sunday before the election.

sections.heading: The Maples Problem
paragraphs: Maples, a financial adviser who branded himself an 'America-First patriot,' faced more than just a Democratic surge. In the weeks before the vote, allegations surfaced that he didn't actually live in the district he was running to represent. Maples denied the claims, but the scrutiny may have undercut his campaign in a race where local credibility mattered.

Gregory, by contrast, ran on kitchen-table issues — healthcare costs, evidence-based policy, and the kind of pragmatic competence that plays well in suburban Palm Beach. The 314 Action political committee, which backs Democratic scientists, endorsed her and declared 'a STEM wave is coming.'

sections.heading: 29 Districts and Counting
paragraphs: Gregory's victory is not an isolated data point. According to the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee, state Democrats have now flipped roughly 29 districts since Trump's election. That's a pattern that extends well beyond Florida — and it has national implications.

'Mar-a-Lago just flipped red to blue, which should have Republicans sweating the midterms,' said DLCC president Heather Williams. DNC chair Ken Martin was blunter: 'If Democrats can win in Trump's backyard, we sure as hell can win anywhere across the country. Onward to November!'

sections.heading: What This Means for November
paragraphs: Midterm elections are scheduled for November, with races for the entire US House of Representatives, a third of the Senate, and multiple governor and state legislature contests. Historically, midterms punish the sitting president's party — and special election results like this suggest the pattern is holding.

The question is whether isolated Democratic wins in special elections translate to a national wave. Republicans will argue that low-turnout specials are unreliable predictors. But a 21-point swing in the district where the president lives is the kind of number that keeps party strategists awake at night.

faqs.q: Who won the Florida District 87 special election?
faqs.a: Democrat Emily Gregory defeated Trump-endorsed Republican Jon Maples by more than 2 percentage points in the special election for Florida's 87th state house district.

faqs.q: How big was the swing in the Mar-a-Lago district?
faqs.a: The district swung roughly 21 points toward Democrats — from a 19-point Republican win in 2024 to a Democratic victory in 2026.

faqs.q: Did Trump vote in the Florida special election?
faqs.a: Yes, Trump voted via mail-in ballot despite publicly criticizing mail-in voting as 'cheating' the same week.

faqs.q: How many districts have Democrats flipped since Trump took office?
faqs.a: Democrats have flipped approximately 29 state-level districts in special elections since Trump's second term began, according to the DLCC.
The 2026 midterm elections are scheduled for November, featuring races for the US House, Senate, and numerous state offices.
